vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF #1046 -- Install/refresh contract self-healing cohort (ADR Choice C). Refined 2026-05-11; absorbs #1045 (PR-A) and #1047 (PR-C); PR-B ships task framework:doctor + canonical manifest + v3 sentinel.

plan.title: Install/refresh contract has no self-healing path (#1046 cohort) -- canonical-path enforcement + framework:doctor + vbrief:preflight resolver

narratives.Description: Install/refresh contract has no self-healing path: agent gets stuck in a no-op Case G loop when install layout disagrees with AGENTS.md
narratives.Overview:
# Directive: install / refresh contract has no self-healing path — agent gets stuck in a no-op loop when install layout disagrees with `AGENTS.md`

## Summary

A real Directive install (`v0.27.1`, fetched by `oz-agent-upgrade` into `<project>/.deft/core/`) put an agent into an unrecoverable Case G loop: `AGENTS.md` references `deft/skills/...` paths that don't resolve, `QUICK-START.md` is one directory deeper than the template assumes, and Case G's "refresh the managed section" prescription is a byte-for-byte no-op against the current template, so re-running just re-detects the same staleness next session.

The right behavior is for Directive itself to detect this and either repair it or surface a clear, actionable error. Today it does neither — it routes the agent through Case G in good faith, the agent does what it's told, and nothing changes.

This issue captures every framework gap surfaced by walking through this single install. They're related (they all live on the install/refresh contract surface) so filing as one issue per maintainer request; happy to split into per-gap children if preferred.

## Reproduction (observed in the wild)

1. New consumer repo. Agent (or human) runs the Oz upgrade pathway. Result: `<project>/.deft-version`, `<project>/.deft/VERSION`, and the full framework checked out under `<project>/.deft/core/`. `AGENTS.md` written at the project root from `templates/agents-entry.md`.
2. `AGENTS.md` says *"Deft is installed in `deft/`. Full guidelines: `deft/main.md`"* and every routing reference is `deft/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, `deft/scripts/...`, `deft/Taskfile.yml`.
3. Next agent session loads `AGENTS.md` and follows it.
4. Per `AGENTS.md`'s own fallback rule (*"If any `deft/skills/` path referenced in this file cannot be read … read `deft/QUICK-START.md` instead and follow it"*) — but `deft/QUICK-START.md` also doesn't exist. Only `.deft/core/QUICK-START.md` exists. Already at this point the agent has to improvise; the fallback is self-referentially broken when the install isn't at `deft/`.
5. If the agent finds `.deft/core/QUICK-START.md` anyway and runs it, Step 2b ("Does `../AGENTS.md` reference any `deft/skills/` path that does not exist on disk?") triggers → Case G.
6. Case G says: replace the managed section with the current content of `./templates/agents-entry.md`. The current `AGENTS.md` managed section is already byte-identical to that template. Refresh is a no-op. Case G's success message ("✓ Refreshed Deft-managed section of AGENTS.md") gets emitted; the underlying problem (install at `.deft/core/` vs. references at `deft/`) is unchanged.
7. New session re-detects the same staleness → infinite Case G loop.

## Findings

### 1. AGENTS.md hard-codes the install path; install path is not actually fixed

`templates/agents-entry.md` literally says *"Deft is installed in `deft/`. Full guidelines: `deft/main.md`"* and every routing line is `deft/...`. But the install location is not contractually fixed:

- An existing pending vBRIEF, `2026-04-23-52-install-into-deft-hidden-directory-instead-of-deft.vbrief.json`, proposes installing at `.deft/` as a feature.
- `oz-agent-upgrade` ships installs at `.deft/core/` today (provenance recorded in `.deft/VERSION`).
- `setup.go::WriteAgentsMD` (referenced from `QUICK-START.md` Case G step 2) writes a template that bakes `deft/` into every routing line.

Two parts of the framework disagree about where Directive lives. Until they agree, *any* non-`deft/` install will produce the loop above.

**Fix shape (for discussion):** parameterize the install path. Either resolve the path at install time and substitute into the template (`<INSTALL_PATH>/skills/...`), or commit to a single canonical path (`deft/`) and have the installer fail loudly if it can't use it.

### 2. QUICK-START Case G refresh is a silent no-op when the bug is layout, not template version

Case G is designed for the v0.19 → v0.20 path-shape upgrade (managed section gets rewritten from the new template). It's not designed for "the install location itself diverges from the template". The detection in Step 2b ("path doesn't exist") fires for both cases, but the prescribed remedy only works for the first.

Today's behavior in the layout-mismatch case:
- AGENTS.md managed section ≡ `templates/agents-entry.md` byte-for-byte.
- Case G rewrites identical bytes back.
- Reports success.
- Next session re-detects.

**Fix shape:** in Step 2b, additionally distinguish "template content differs" vs. "template content matches but install path doesn't resolve". The latter is a different failure class (Case K — install location mismatch?) and should not be routed through "refresh the managed section".

### 3. No `task framework:doctor` / `task install:verify`

`task framework:check-updates` exists (remote version probe, issue #801). There is no analogous *local* integrity probe. The diagnostic needed for self-healing is:

- Does `<install>/QUICK-START.md` resolve from the path AGENTS.md claims?
- Does every `deft/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` referenced by AGENTS.md resolve?
- Does `<install>/VERSION` agree with `<project-root>/.deft-version`?
- Is the install location consistent across `AGENTS.md`, the installer manifest, and the on-disk reality?
- Is the install committed to the consumer repo, ignored, or a submodule? (See finding #5.)

This is the missing surface where "self-healing" lives. Without it, the only feedback loop is `agent enters Case G → agent emits identical bytes → agent claims success → session ends → next agent enters Case G`.

**Fix shape:** add `task framework:doctor` that runs the above checks and either repairs (rewrites AGENTS.md path tokens to the detected install path) or emits a structured remediation prescription the agent can act on. Wire it into AGENTS.md's session-start routing ahead of Step 2b.

### 4. `<project-root>/.deft-version` duplicates `<install>/VERSION`

In this install, both exist:

- `<project-root>/.deft-version` (untracked):
  ```
  0.27.1
  ```
- `<project-root>/.deft/VERSION` (tracked):
  ```
  ref: 'v0.27.1'
  sha: 'af829f4ec5bbe6ef562722d76080bb94ba893f8e'
  tag: 'v0.27.1'
  fetched_at: '2026-05-11T15:30:52Z'
  fetched_by: 'oz-agent-upgrade'
  ```

Two source-of-truth files, different shapes (bare version vs. YAML-ish provenance), opposite tracking states. They will drift. Doctor needs to know which is canonical and reconcile or alias.

**Fix shape:** pick one canonical install manifest (recommend the richer YAML provenance file). Have the installer write only that. If the bare `.deft-version` is needed for compat, generate it from the manifest at install time and mark it as a derivative.

### 5. Installer silently commits itself into the consumer repo

`git ls-files .deft | wc -l` in this consumer repo returns *thousands* of files. The default `.gitignore` does not exclude `.deft/` (or `deft/`). Working-tree state on first inspection: every file in `.deft/core/` is in `M` state because Oz wrote on top of an older committed copy. The agent's first attempt to run Case G is blocked by a dirty tree that is itself a framework artifact, not user content.

This was almost certainly not a designed outcome — a consumer install should be one of: gitignored, a git submodule, or a deliberately vendored copy with explicit user opt-in. Today's behavior is "vendor by default, silently, and let dirty-tree symptoms surface to agents downstream".

**Fix shape:** installer should (a) detect that the install target is inside a git repo, (b) ask the user up front which of {gitignore, submodule, vendor-and-commit} they want, (c) record the decision somewhere the doctor can verify on re-entry, and (d) refuse to silently overwrite a previously-committed install of a different version (which is what produced the M-state mess here).

### 6. Case G's "Start a new session" instruction has no enforcement

Case G step 5 says *"Framework updated. Start a new agent session to pick up the changes. The current session has stale context. Do not continue past this instruction in the current session."* This is a plea, not a guarantee. An agent (or a human in agentic mode) that ignores it gets indistinguishable behavior from a successful refresh. There's no token Directive can check on re-entry that says "this AGENTS.md was refreshed at sha X at time T, did the session restart?".

**Fix shape:** stamp the managed-section sentinel with the framework sha and refresh timestamp (e.g. `<!-- deft:managed-section v2 sha=af829f4 refreshed=2026-05-11T16:00:00Z -->`). On re-entry, if a Case-G refresh was performed in the same session lineage without a session boundary, escalate to the user instead of silently re-running Case G.

### 7. Implementation Intent Gate (#810) hard-codes `deft/scripts/preflight_implementation.py`

The managed section says:
> Run `task vbrief:preflight -- <path>` before any code-writing tool call or `start_agent` dispatch -- the gate exits 0 only when … The Taskfile target wraps `deft/scripts/preflight_implementation.py` …

Same hard-coded `deft/` path. If the install is at `.deft/core/`, the Taskfile target is unrunnable. The gate then silently fails open — which is the opposite of what #810 was supposed to provide (a hard pre-implementation gate). Worse, this is a *safety* gate, not a routing gate, so its silent failure-open is materially more dangerous than the other path mismatches in this issue.

**Fix shape:** every safety/gate doc reference needs to resolve through whatever install-path indirection #1 lands on, *and* the gate Taskfile target needs to fail-closed if the wrapped script can't be located. Better to surface a loud "gate is misconfigured, refusing to proceed" than to pass silently.

## Cross-cutting fix proposal (for the discussion you said comes next)

These seven findings collapse to one architectural ask: **install path must be a parameter, not a literal, throughout the agent-facing contract surface**. Once that's true:

- Doctor can verify it (finding #3 resolves).
- Case G can detect path-mismatch vs. content-mismatch (finding #2 resolves).
- Hard-coded `deft/` strings in AGENTS.md template and #810 go away (findings #1, #7 resolve).
- Manifest/version files have one canonical owner that knows the install path (finding #4 resolves).
- Installer can ask "where, and how do you want this tracked?" once and persist it (finding #5 resolves).
- Refresh stamping (finding #6) becomes a property of the resolved managed section, not a separate concern.

Happy to spec this as an ADR or vBRIEF if that's the right next step.

## Provenance

- Consumer repo: `deftai/postmortem` @ `master`
- Install version: `v0.27.1` (sha `af829f4ec5bbe6ef562722d76080bb94ba893f8e`)
- Install path: `<consumer-root>/.deft/core/`
- Install agent: `oz-agent-upgrade` @ `2026-05-11T15:30:52Z`
- Discovered during: routine `use agents.md` session-start routing on the postmortem consumer repo.
narratives.Problem: Seven coupled findings in the install/refresh contract collapse to one architectural defect: install path is a literal (`deft/`) baked into the agent-facing contract, but the install location is not contractually fixed. `oz-agent-upgrade` ships at `.deft/core/`; the pending vBRIEF #52 proposes `.deft/`; the v0.27 #992 PR1 contract-string flip and PR3 cmd_gate auto-prompt pin `.deft/core/` as canonical. Wherever the literal does not match the install reality, agents loop in Case G (byte-identical refresh of a managed section that is the SOURCE of the mismatch, not the symptom). The Implementation Intent Gate (#810) safety surface fails open silently on the same defect class because the Taskfile target wraps a hardcoded `deft/scripts/preflight_implementation.py` path.
narratives.Constraint: Per #992 the v0.27 release line pinned `.deft/core/` as the single-namespace canonical install target. Choice C (this ADR) accepts that pin -- v0.28 enforces canonical-path + fail-loud across the contract surface; true parameterization (Choice A) is deferred to v0.30+ unless a second install layout becomes a real requirement. NOT in scope: installer consent gate (Finding #5; UX-significant, separate scope, filed as a follow-up). NOT in scope: `deft/` -> something-else rename (#418/#423 deferred). MUST NOT regress #1035 Tier 2.5 detector or #1019 destructive-gh-verb gate. MUST keep managed-section sentinel parsing back-compatible during the v2 -> v3 marker bump (recognise both for one release cycle, per the v0.20 `<!-- deft:deprecated-redirect -->` precedent).
narratives.Outcome: Three coordinated PRs land sequentially on master (strict rebase-cascade, no parallel within cluster). PR-A flips every `deft/` literal in `templates/agents-entry.md` to `.deft/core/` and adds a new Case K classifier to `run::_classify_install_layout` so layout-mismatch fail-loud instead of silent-no-op Case G. PR-B ships `task framework:doctor` (four checks: QUICK-START resolves; every skill path resolves; `<install>/VERSION` agrees with `<root>/.deft-version`; install path consistent across AGENTS.md / on-disk / manifest), picks `<install>/VERSION` (YAML provenance) as canonical install manifest with `.deft-version` as a derivative, and bumps the managed-section sentinel to v3 with `sha=<framework-sha> refreshed=<iso> session=<id>` for Case-G session-boundary enforcement. PR-C rewrites `scripts/preflight_implementation.py` resolution to probe `.deft/core/scripts/preflight_implementation.py` first, then `deft/scripts/...`, then in-repo `scripts/...`; `tasks/vbrief.yml::preflight` consumes the resolver; fail-closed when no candidate resolves (exit non-zero with `gate misconfigured -- run task framework:doctor` pointer); AGENTS.md managed-section text rewritten to drop the path-handling claim and refer to the resolved Taskfile contract.
narratives.Test: PR-A: `tests/cmd_gate/test_case_k.py` -- A->K state transitions, byte-identical template + missing install dir, byte-different template + present install dir; existing `tests/cmd_gate/test_state_detection.py` 28 tests preserved. PR-B: `tests/cli/test_framework_doctor.py` -- per-check pass/fail matrix (QUICK-START resolve, every skill path resolve, VERSION-vs-.deft-version drift, AGENTS.md path-token consistency), three-state exit-code contract; `tests/cli/test_managed_section_v3.py` -- v3 sentinel parse, sha/refreshed/session field extraction, v2-back-compat during deprecation window, session-boundary re-entry escalation. PR-C: `tests/cli/test_vbrief_preflight_resolver.py` -- .deft/core probe wins, `deft/` legacy fallback, in-repo `scripts/` fallback, all-missing fail-closed exit code, structured error message contract. All three PRs: `task check` MUST exit 0; #1035 Tier 2.5 detector regression suite continues to pass; closing-keyword lint clean on PR body + commit message.
narratives.DesignChoice: Choice C (Hybrid: pin canonical now, parameterize later) selected over Choice A (parameterize everywhere) and Choice B (pin and fail-loud, no doctor surface). Rationale: solves the seven reproduced findings with the smallest code-surface change; matches the existing #992 release-line pin; defers the architectural cost of parameterization until a second install layout actually exists as a requirement. The agile-over-enterprise instinct -- solve the bug in front of us, do not over-design for hypothetical install layouts that have not surfaced. If a second install layout DOES surface post-v0.28, escalate to Choice A as a follow-up cohort, NOT a mid-cohort scope expansion.
narratives.Urgency: HOT. The Implementation Intent Gate failure mode (Finding #7 / PR-C) is a safety-significant silent-fail on every `.deft/core/` install. Every swarm worker dispatched on a state-B consumer is currently in a position where its safety gate is unreachable but its contract says the gate is in force. The Case G no-op loop (Findings #1, #2 / PR-A) is operationally serious -- it burns one full session per agent that hits it. PR-A and PR-C are higher priority than PR-B; PR-B is the diagnostic surface that PR-A and PR-C will need post-landing.

items.title: PR-A: flip every `deft/` literal in `templates/agents-entry.md` to `.deft/core/` (absorbs #1045)

narrative.Action: Audit `templates/agents-entry.md` for every `deft/` substring (including `deft/skills/...`, `deft/main.md`, `deft/QUICK-START.md`, `deft/scripts/...`, `deft/Taskfile.yml`, `deft/run`). Flip to `.deft/core/...`. Update the fallback rule pointer from `deft/QUICK-START.md` to `.deft/core/QUICK-START.md`. Pin with a regression test under `tests/contract/test_no_legacy_deft_paths_in_agents_template.py` analogous to `tests/contract/test_no_legacy_deft_run.py`. Closing-keyword in PR body: `Closes #1045` (absorbed; the issue is fixed by PR-A landing). Does NOT touch the Implementation Intent Gate copy (PR-C territory).

items.title: PR-A: add Case K (install location mismatch) to `run::_classify_install_layout`

narrative.Action: Extend the A/B/C/D classifier in `run::_classify_install_layout` with a new Case K = template content byte-matches AND install path does not resolve. New helpers: `_install_path_resolves(project_root) -> bool`, `_template_matches_managed_section(project_root) -> bool`. Update `QUICK-START.md` Step 2b to split staleness into (i) template-differs -> existing Case G (preserved verbatim) and (ii) path-doesn't-resolve -> new Case K -> fail-loud message naming the install-path mismatch and pointing at `task framework:doctor` (PR-B's surface). Detector remains read-only -- no filesystem mutation. Tests in `tests/cmd_gate/test_case_k.py`.

Pure stdlib, three-state exit (0 clean / 1 drift detected / 2 config error), mirrors `scripts/preflight_branch.py` (#747) and `scripts/preflight_gh.py` (#1019) shape with the #814 UTF-8 self-reconfigure at `main()` entry. Four checks: (1) `<install>/QUICK-START.md` resolves from the path AGENTS.md claims; (2) every `<install>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` referenced by AGENTS.md resolves; (3) `<install>/VERSION` agrees with `<root>/.deft-version` if the bare file exists; (4) install path consistent across AGENTS.md / on-disk / manifest. New `tasks/framework.yml::doctor` Taskfile target (no `sources:` / `generates:` per #574). Wire into `run::_check_upgrade_gate` after the existing #801 remote-version probe; doctor is informational on its own (does not block) but its diagnostics feed PR-A's Case K classifier. Tests in `tests/cli/test_framework_doctor.py`.

items.title: PR-B: pin `<install>/VERSION` (YAML provenance) as canonical install manifest, `.deft-version` as derivative

narrative.Action: Pick the YAML provenance file (`ref` / `sha` / `tag` / `fetched_at` / `fetched_by` shape, already produced by `oz-agent-upgrade`) as the canonical install manifest. `cmd_install` and `cmd_upgrade` write only the YAML file; `.deft-version` (bare version) becomes a derivative regenerated at install time. Doctor check #3 verifies the two are in agreement; on drift, prefer the YAML manifest and regenerate the derivative. Document the new contract in `docs/install-manifest.md`. UPGRADING.md gets a new section covering the transition for v0.27.x consumers carrying only the bare file.

items.title: PR-B: bump managed-section sentinel v2 -> v3 with sha/refreshed/session tokens (session-boundary enforcement)

narrative.Action: Marker bump: `<!-- deft:managed-section v2 -->` -> `<!-- deft:managed-section v3 sha=<framework-sha> refreshed=<iso> session=<id> -->`. `cmd_agents_refresh` writes the v3 marker with the current framework sha, ISO 8601 UTC refresh timestamp, and a synthesized session id (uuid4 -- ephemeral per refresh). On re-entry, `_check_upgrade_gate` parses the v3 marker; if Case G refreshed within the same session lineage (by some inbound session-id mechanism the agent runtime supplies, OR by absence of a session-boundary transition token) the gate escalates to the user instead of silently re-running Case G (resolves Finding #6). Back-compat: v2 markers continue to parse for one release cycle (deprecation window). Tests in `tests/cli/test_managed_section_v3.py`.

items.title: PR-C: `vbrief:preflight` Taskfile target uses resolver + fail-closed (absorbs #1047)

narrative.Action: Rewrite `scripts/preflight_implementation.py` resolution chain: probe `.deft/core/scripts/preflight_implementation.py` first, fall back to `deft/scripts/preflight_implementation.py` (state-A legacy), fall back to `scripts/preflight_implementation.py` (in-repo case). `tasks/vbrief.yml::preflight` consumes the resolver via `{{joinPath .TASKFILE_DIR ".."}}` pattern. ⊗ Fail-closed: if no candidate resolves, exit non-zero with structured error `gate misconfigured: cannot resolve preflight_implementation.py at any expected path -- run task framework:doctor`. AGENTS.md managed-section text rewritten to drop the path-handling claim (`whether deft is the project root or installed as a deft/ subdirectory`) and refer to the resolved Taskfile contract instead. Closing-keyword in PR body: `Closes #1047` (absorbed). Audit `tasks/*.yml` for any other `deft/...` literals wrapping safety-relevant scripts; apply the same fix. Tests in `tests/cli/test_vbrief_preflight_resolver.py`.

references.title: Issue #1046: Install/refresh contract has no self-healing path (this scope)
Issue #1045: templates/agents-entry.md body still references deft/ (ABSORBED by PR-A)
Issue #1047: Implementation Intent Gate Taskfile target hardcodes deft/scripts/preflight_implementation.py (ABSORBED by PR-C)
Issue #810: Implementation Intent Gate (the gate whose Taskfile target PR-C rewrites)
Issue #801: remote-version probe via git ls-remote (precedent for the doctor-side gate wiring)
Issue #768: universal upgrade gate + AGENTS.md contract (defines the managed-section marker contract PR-B v3-bumps)
Issue #992: adopt .deft/core/ canonical install layout (v0.27 cohort; PR1 marker bump + PR3 cmd_gate state detector + PR4 contract-string flip)
Issue #52: install into .deft/ hidden directory (pending vBRIEF that informed the install-layout-not-fixed observation)
Issue #747: detection-bound branch gate (shape precedent for scripts/framework_doctor.py)
Issue #1019: destructive-gh-verb gate (shape precedent for scripts/framework_doctor.py)
Issue #814: UTF-8 self-reconfigure pattern at main() entry (applies to scripts/framework_doctor.py)
Issue #1035: poller detector Tier 2.5 (sibling cohort -- MUST NOT regress)
Issue #1039: poller STALL terminal exit (sibling cohort -- MUST NOT regress)
Issue #1050: installer consent gate (#1046 Finding #5 follow-up, DEFERRED to v0.29+)

x-tracking.design_choice: Choice C (pin canonical now, parameterize later)
x-tracking.design_choice_rationale: Solves the seven reproduced findings with the smallest code-surface change; matches the existing #992 release-line pin; defers true parameterization to v0.30+ unless a second install layout becomes a real requirement.

absorbs.rationale: templates/agents-entry.md literal flip is PR-A AC-1. (AC-2 Case K classifier addresses Finding #2 from #1046 itself, NOT #1045.)
preflight resolver + fail-closed is PR-C AC-6.

deferred_to_followup.rationale: UX-significant -- gitignore vs submodule vs vendor-and-commit deserves its own scope vBRIEF + design discussion. Filed as separate issue post-ADR.
Deferred to v0.30+ unless a second install layout becomes a real requirement.
deferred_to_followup.tracking_issue_note: Explicit null -- no tracking issue filed yet. A second install layout has not surfaced as a real requirement; the deferral becomes actionable only when one does. If/when that happens, file a new issue here and update both fields.

x-tracking.branch_strategy: PR-A -> PR-B -> PR-C strict sequential rebase-cascade (no parallel within cluster).

files_must_not_touch: vbrief/PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json (rendered artifact; touch only via task project:render)
ROADMAP.md (rendered artifact; touch only via task roadmap:render)
shared_file_overlap_within_cohort: templates/agents-entry.md (PR-A flips literals; PR-B updates managed-section marker; PR-C rewrites Implementation Intent Gate copy) -- strict sequential rebase-cascade required
run (PR-A adds Case K classifier; PR-B wires doctor probe into _check_upgrade_gate + v3 sentinel parser) -- PR-B rebases on PR-A
